title: Apple and blackberry pie
subtitle: Double-crust shortcrust pie with Bramley apple and blackberries
excerpt: A deep double-crust pie filled with Bramley apples and fresh blackberries, scented with cinnamon and lemon. The pastry is made with cold butter and rested before rolling. The filling is cooked briefly before going into the case so the fruit cooks evenly. Serves six.
sourceNotes: Apple pie is one of the oldest documented British baked preparations. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: an apple pie made with shortcrust pastry, cooking apples, and sugar is documented as a foundational British preparation. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: a pastry-lidded apple pie with blackberries or black currants added for flavour is documented as a seasonal variation. The blackberry addition is consistent with the autumn fruit season documented in British domestic cookery throughout the nineteenth century.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unbaked assembled pie. Bake from frozen, adding 15 minutes to the bake time.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The filling can be made a day ahead and refrigerated. Assemble and bake on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the crust should be golden and the filling should be bubbling through the vent holes; 40–45 minutes

content.text: Pre-cooking the apple filling prevents the top crust from doming away from the fruit as the raw apple shrinks during baking. Add the blackberries at the end of the pre-cook so they soften slightly without disintegrating. Leave the filling to cool completely before assembly or the steam will make the bottom crust soggy.

items.prepNote: about 700 g, peeled, cored, and sliced 1 cm thick

content.text: Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}} (pastry portion), {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{caster-sugar}} (pastry portion) until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs with some larger pieces remaining. Add 4 to 5 tablespoons of cold water and press together until the dough just comes together; do not overwork. Divide in two (one slightly larger for the base), flatten into discs, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Toss the {{apple-bramley}} with the {{caster-sugar}} (filling portion), {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{lemon}} juice, and {{plain-flour}} (filling portion) in a medium saucepan. Cook over medium heat for 5 to 7 minutes, stirring, until the apple softens slightly but still holds its shape. Stir in the {{blackberries}} and cook for 2 minutes more. Remove from the heat and cool completely.

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Roll the larger pastry disc to 3 mm thick and line a 23 cm pie dish, leaving a 2 cm overhang. Add the cooled filling. Roll the second disc and lay over the filling. Trim and crimp the edges together to seal. Cut 3 or 4 small slits in the top to vent steam. Brush with the beaten {{eggs}} and scatter a little caster sugar over the top. Bake on the middle shelf for 40 to 45 minutes until the pastry is deep gold and the filling is bubbling through the vents. Rest for 20 minutes before serving.
